partition_law: every finding of the six audit classes is assigned to exactly one disposition class by the selectors below; the validator recomputes this partition from the audit bytes on every run and refuses drift
single_wave_consequence: under the no-same-ID-mutation law the blast radius is one coordinated reissue wave over the touched-schema union; accepting or rejecting classes changes reissued content, never the wave size
sequencing: any D5 new-domain registration and any profile-core edit first, because they re-freeze the core bytes
D9 profile-binding pins against the final core bytes
the D1 fixture wave plus fixture URN repins
audit regeneration must then show all six blocking findings at zero; refreshing the audit without resolving findings is not closure

defs_comparator.rule: skeletons mask scalar constraint values, enum contents, and const values; enum lists and const values form the vocabulary. One shared skeleton and one vocabulary is D6; one skeleton with differing vocabularies is D8; differing skeletons is D7. Constraint-keyword presence is treated as structure, the conservative reading; the presence-insensitive triage is recorded so the sensitivity is visible rather than silent

classes.title: Fixture timestamp normalization
classes.recommendation: normalize every value to the frozen UTC microsecond lexical form and regenerate the audit corpus digests; zero schema reissues

classes.title: Datetime profile pinning
classes.recommendation: pin each path to one shared profiled timestamp definition and reissue; one enumeration pass first for any source-reported external-time exemption

classes.title: Scientific-decimal typed-object migration
classes.recommendation: migrate each field to the frozen typed scientific-decimal object; the semantic type, unit, and precision per row require operator approval of the embedded table

classes.title: Binary-number domain declaration
classes.recommendation: declare a frozen bounded-integer domain or migrate to decimal strings; both findings are in research-mission.schema.json

classes.title: Digest scope annotation
classes.selector: every schemas[].digest_fields[] entry with scope_annotation_present false
classes.recommendation: annotate each field via shared scoped-digest definitions distinguishing byte digests from canonical object digests; rows proposing a domain beyond the frozen 21 require separate operator judgment and re-freeze the profile core

classes.title: Divergent definitions: constraint-only, converge
classes.selector: divergent_common_definitions entries whose variants share one structural skeleton and one enum vocabulary under the comparator in defs_comparator
classes.recommendation: converge to one canonical shared definition per name; every narrowing carries a known-bad vector proving the stricter form refuses what the profile intends to refuse

classes.title: Divergent definitions: structural
classes.selector: divergent_common_definitions entries whose variants differ structurally under the comparator in defs_comparator
classes.recommendation: rename-distinct by default; converge-to-superset only where provably safe, decided per name over the retained variant diffs

classes.title: Divergent definitions: enum vocabulary policy
classes.selector: divergent_common_definitions entries whose variants share one skeleton but differ in enum vocabularies under the comparator in defs_comparator
classes.recommendation: closed central vocabularies with declared per-context subsets

classes.title: Profile-binding pinning
classes.recommendation: pin the exact prospective profile reference members only after the profile core bytes are final; any later core edit invalidates every pin

d5_field_table.proposed_subject_class_or_domain: four-way split: artifact/profile refs are byte digests; checkpoint/event/receipt refs use existing domains; ~70 refs target NEW record domains; ~72 generic-reference defs resolve per use site
d5_field_table.note: the only name spanning byte and canonical in one shared def family; split is mandatory at reissue

boundary: architecture evidence only; accepting any class authorizes candidate reissue work, never admission, canonical digests, runtime, or Gate A

proposal_provenance.method: every D3 pointer and D5 field name was read in its schema context by a proposing pass; nothing was inferred from the field name alone. The same pass exposed the audit's decimal-detector defect corrected by ADR 0033.
proposal_provenance.boundary: proposals only; nothing here is accepted, and the operator decides every class, every table row marked needs_operator, and every new domain
